items.title: Form submission model: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents misconception? The form element automatically sends every visible control even when it lacks a name.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Only successful named controls are submitted; choose method and action from request semantics and validate again on the server.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Only successful named controls are submitted; choose method and action from request semantics and validate again on the server.

items.title: Form submission model: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Use form, action, method, names and values while distinguishing GET and POST semantics. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating GET form with successful named controls; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.
